name: config
context: Configure drizzle-kit via drizzle.config.ts — dialect, schema path, output directory, and database credentials
info: The `drizzle.config.ts` file is the central configuration for drizzle-kit CLI commands (generate, migrate, push, pull, studio). It uses `defineConfig()` from `drizzle-kit` and specifies the database dialect (postgresql, mysql, sqlite, turso, singlestore, mssql), schema file path(s), output directory for migrations, and database credentials. Credentials can be a URL string or individual host/port/user/password/database fields. Optional settings include `strict` (confirmation for destructive changes), `verbose` (print SQL), `tablesFilter` (glob-based table filtering), `schemaFilter` (PostgreSQL schema names), `migrations` (custom migration table name and schema), `breakpoints` (statement separators), and `introspect.casing` (column name casing on pull). Environment variables are commonly used for credentials via `process.env.DATABASE_URL`. Multiple config files support different environments.

sections.title: Required configuration options
sections.body: `dialect` sets the database type: `postgresql`, `mysql`, `sqlite`, `turso`, `singlestore`, `mssql`. `schema` is a path (string or glob) to your TypeScript schema files. These two are required for all drizzle-kit commands. `dbCredentials` is required for commands that touch the database (migrate, push, pull, studio) but not for `generate` (which only reads schema files and snapshots).

sections.title: Migration output directory
sections.body: The `out` option (default: `'drizzle'`) sets where migration files, snapshots, and the journal are stored. This directory should be committed to version control. All drizzle-kit commands that read or write migrations use this path.

sections.title: Table and schema filters
sections.body: `tablesFilter` accepts glob patterns to limit which tables drizzle-kit manages — useful for shared databases or multi-tenant setups. `schemaFilter` limits to specific PostgreSQL schemas (default: all in 1.0+). `extensionsFilters` prevents extension tables (like PostGIS geometry_columns) from appearing in migrations.

sections.title: Multiple configuration files
sections.body: Pass `--config=path/to/config.ts` to any drizzle-kit command to use an alternate config. Common pattern: `drizzle.dev.config.ts` pointing to a local database and `drizzle.prod.config.ts` pointing to production. This supports per-environment schema management without complex branching logic.
